genre_refs: Make America Great Again (MAGA slogan riff)
description: A Department of Labor designed slogan/headline-collage graphic. The background is a photograph of a welder at work inside an industrial factory: a person wearing a 'VULCAN' welding helmet, white t-shirt and denim/canvas apron, hunched over a metal workpiece with bright blue-white sparks and smoke flaring up; rows of overhead industrial lights and steel structure recede behind. A dark navy gradient overlays the lower half. Centered headline text reads 'Make America' in white italic serif and 'SKILLED AGAIN!' in large bold white serif caps, a deliberate play on the 'Make America Great Again' slogan. Below are three torn-paper-style faux newspaper clippings with outlet logos: HR DIVE - 'DOL to provide $30M in training grants on AI, skilled trades'; POLITICO PRO - 'Trump administration announces new workforce development plan'; and NTD - 'Department of Labor Says College-for-All Model Failed, Unveils New Workforce Plan.' The propaganda technique uses patriotic slogan branding plus curated press headlines to validate the administration's workforce/trade-school agenda and denigrate the traditional four-year-college path. Target audience: working-class Americans, trades workers, and supporters of the administration's labor agenda.
notable_text: Make America SKILLED AGAIN! / DOL to provide $30M in training grants on AI, skilled trades / Trump administration announces new workforce development plan / Department of Labor Says College-for-All Model Failed, Unveils New Workforce Plan
